---
title: "Model-training/fitting"
author: "Aldair Martinez Pineda"
date: "2024-07-03"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(readxl)
library(stats)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggthemes)
library(dplyr)
library(stringi)
library(broom)


predictionrules <- read_excel("predictioncodonH5-vmod.xlsx")
# full mapped data from shim
datafullshim <- read_excel("allinsertions.xlsx")
# full mapped data from 6A set

predictionrules$pos <- predictionrules$pos + 1



```






```{r}
# create the new joined data frames using position, sample and size as grouping variables

datafullshim$sample2 <-
  gsub(".*HA(.+)-p.*", "\\1", datafullshim$sample)
predictionrules$sample2 <- predictionrules$sample
datafullshim$decalage <-
  as.numeric(substr(datafullshim$insertion, 1, 1))
datafullshim$pos <- datafullshim$Realpos

mergedfull <-
  merge(datafullshim,
        predictionrules,
        by = c("pos", "sample2", "decalage"))


# select only positions from 1011 to 1020

mergedfull <-
  mergedfull[mergedfull$Realpos > 1010 & mergedfull$Realpos < 1020, ]

# create an order version of the original insertions in Y insert

for (i in 1:length(mergedfull$insertion.y)) {
  mergedfull$order1[i] <-
    paste(sort(unlist(strsplit(
      mergedfull$insertion.y[i], ""
    ))), collapse = "")
}

# do the same for the insertion x that must be corrected before
mergedfull$insertion.x1 <- substr(mergedfull$insertion.x, 2, 20)

for (i in 1:length(mergedfull$insertion.x1)) {
  mergedfull$order2[i] <-
    paste(sort(unlist(
      strsplit(mergedfull$insertion.x1[i], "")
    )), collapse = "")
}


# now filter out all the inconsistent insertions

mergedfull <- mergedfull[mergedfull$order1 == mergedfull$order2, ]


# create the log freq variable to transform the frequencies of insertions to log scale

mergedfull$logfreq = log10(mergedfull$FreqInser)

# create the adenine and other variables

mergedfull$adenine = 0
mergedfull[mergedfull$order1 == "A", ]$adenine <- 1
# create value for mismatch analysis
mergedfull$match <- 1
mergedfull[substr(mergedfull$structure, 1, 1) == ".", ]$match <- 0

# ready for regression analysis


# create second data frame with the means of insertions to reduce biological replicate noise

mergedfullmean <-
  (
    aggregate(
      logfreq ~ Realpos + sample2 + decalage + adenine + match + neighbor + DDG + deltaG + insertion.x1 + insertion.y + order1 +  neighborblock + pathway + equilibrated + matchmismatch,
      data = mergedfull,
      FUN = mean,
      na.rm = TRUE
    )
  )

# limit the neighbor variable for short insertions



```



```{r}



# create first model to verify data integrity

# Exclude ShimA3 AA and AAA insertion events because their orientation cannot be
# assigned unambiguously to vRNA or cRNA synthesis. These observations are therefore
# not suitable for a model that links insertion frequency to orientation-specific
# product-template duplex parameters.


mergedfullmean <-
  mergedfullmean[mergedfullmean$sample2 != "ShimA3" |
                   (mergedfullmean$insertion.y != "AA" &
                      mergedfullmean$insertion.y != "AAA"), ]


# PR8-C6A low-frequency constraints.
# These insertion classes correspond to strongly penalized product-template
# configurations predicted by DDG and match/mismatch status. They were included
# to constrain the low-frequency end of the model, where such events are expected
# to occur below or close to the reliable detection range.
#
# The values represent conservative low-frequency estimates rather than independent
# biological replicates; they are repeated three times to give appropriate weight
# to this experimentally informative constraint during model fitting.



falsepositives <-
  data.frame(
    Realpos = c(1012, 1012),
    sample2 = c("PR8-C6A", "PR8-C6A"),
    decalage = c(2, 3),
    adenine = c(0, 0),
    match = c(0, 0),
    neighbor = c(11, 8),
    DDG = c(2.3, 2.9),
    deltaG = c(-4.2, -3.6),
    insertion.x1 = c("CA", "CAA"),
    insertion.y = c("CA", "CAA"),
    order1 = c("A", "A"),
    neighborblock = c(8, 2),
    pathway = c(1, 1),
    equilibrated = c(1, 1),
    matchmismatch = c("D", "D"),
    logfreq = c(-5.1,-5.2)
  )


# inflate the falsepositives

falsepositives <-
  falsepositives[rep(seq_len(nrow(falsepositives)), 3),]




# create the linear regression model
# set the reference variables

mergedfullmean$order1 <- as.factor(mergedfullmean$order1)

# now to set the references
mergedfullmean$order1 <- relevel(mergedfullmean$order1, ref = "A")


# codify levels for predicted variables

mergedfullmean$matchmismatch <- factor(mergedfullmean$matchmismatch,
                                       levels = unique(mergedfullmean$matchmismatch))

# Restrict model training to insertion frequencies above the reliable
# quantification threshold. At lower frequencies, the finite sequencing depth
# causes substantial sampling variability, preventing robust estimation of the
# underlying insertion-frequency distribution.
#
# The model is therefore calibrated on reproducible quantifiable insertion events,
# rather than on rare observations dominated by stochastic sampling noise.



mergedfullmean2 <-
  rbind(mergedfullmean[mergedfullmean$logfreq > -4.4, ], falsepositives)




Model_linReg3.1 <-
  lm(logfreq ~  DDG + matchmismatch,  data = mergedfullmean2)


# visualize the model results

summary(Model_linReg3.1)


# saveRDS(Model_linReg3.1, file = "Model_linReg3.1.rds")


```



```{r, fig.height=5,  fig.width=8}



####first analysis challenging model vs biological variation
#visualize the good model



#add the model column to merged dataframe



windowsFonts(Arial = windowsFont("Arial"))




mergedfullmean2$predicted31 <-
  predict.lm(Model_linReg3.1, mergedfullmean2)




data.plot3 <-
  ggplot(mergedfullmean2, aes(x = predicted31, y = logfreq, color = sample2)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = order1), size = 5) + geom_abline(lty = 2,
                                                         color = "gray60",
                                                         linewidth = 1.5) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(
    x = "predicted frequency log10",
    y = "observed frequency log10",
    color = NULL,
    title = "Predicted mutation frequency vs real mutation frequency model with false negatives",
  ) + theme_tufte() + theme(
    axis.line = element_line(),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      vjust = 0.5,
      hjust = 1
    ),
    text = element_text(size = 13, family = "Arial")
  )

data.plot3


ggsave(
  data.plot3,
  filename = "predicted-vs-observed-insertion-Shimdata.png",
  device = "png",
  dpi = 600,
  height = 8,
  width = 12,
  units = "in"
)

```
